In March, O’Hara posthumously won an Actor Award for outstanding performance by a female actor in a comedy series for her work on “The Studio.” Co-star Seth Rogen raved about her when he accepted it on her behalf.

“Something that I’ve just been marveling at over the last few weeks was really her ability to be generous and kind and gracious while never, ever minimizing her own talents and her own ability to contribute to the work that we were doing,” he said.
“She knew she could destroy, and she wanted to destroy every day on set.”

title: EXCLUSIVE: 'Baby-Sitters Club' Author Shares a New Book Coming Out for 40-Year Anniversary
summary: Four decades since the first book of “The Baby-Sitters Club” series came out, creator and author Ann M. Martin is looking back.“When I set out to write the first book in The Baby-Sitters Club, 'Kristy’s Great Idea,' I wanted to create a series about girls who were strong, entrepreneurial, independent thinkers, and problem-solvers — and friends who were kind, compassionate, and supportive of one another despite their many differences."Now, nearly four decades later, I receive letters fr
story: Four decades since the first book of “The Baby-Sitters Club” series came out, creator and author Ann M. Martin is looking back.

“When I set out to write the first book in The Baby-Sitters Club, 'Kristy’s Great Idea,' I wanted to create a series about girls who were strong, entrepreneurial, independent thinkers, and problem-solvers — and friends who were kind, compassionate, and supportive of one another despite their many differences.

"Now, nearly four decades later, I receive letters from kids who say their parents introduced them to the series and that they enjoy reading the books together. I love the idea of parents, caregivers, and children bonding over the stories," she says.
The first "Baby-sitters Club" book led to a total of 250 books, 200 million copies sold, shows on HBO and Netflix, a film and now, another graphic novel addition.

As part of the book series’ 40-year anniversary, Scholastic is releasing the first-ever graphic novel adaptation of a “Baby-sitters Club” super special book. The 15 “super special” books are longer than the standard novels and feature narration from every member of the BSC.
“The Baby-sitters Club Super Special: Baby-sitters on Board,” adapted by Raina Telgemeier and Gale Galligan, comes out on Sept. 15.

In the book, Kristy, Claudia, Mary Anne, Stacey, Dawn, Mallory and Jessi head to the Bahamas on a cruise for three days of fun. Amid all the adventure, these girls still have time for what they do best: babysitting.

“The graphic novel adaptations are a wonderful addition to the series,” Martin said in a statement to TODAY.com. “I’m in awe of how a story can be carried forward through illustrations — even by something as seemingly simple as a facial expression.”
New York Times bestselling authors Raina Telgemeier and Gale Galligan, who previously collaborated on BSC graphic novels, say the opportunity feels like a full-circle moment.
Telgemeier told TODAY.com in a statement that she "fell in love" with the BSC in 1986.

"My fondness for the girls and the world of Stoneybrook is as strong as ever," Telgemeier reveals. "What a joy to pick up my pencil and return to illustrating Ann’s characters, and what a thrill to share this ride with Gale — who is such a force of cartooning nature themself!"
Galligan, too, shares a personal connection to the BSC storyline and one character in particular.

“From the moment I met Claudia Kishi and discovered that there were books about Asian American kids like me, to the moment I saw my own graphic novel adaptation on a bookshelf for the first time, 'The Baby-sitters Club' has opened my life up in ways I could never have imagined," Galligan said.
"I’m delighted to celebrate its 40th anniversary in the most BSC way possible: by collaborating with dear friends," Galligan added.
Martin echoes the book' sisterhood. She says "stepping into the world of Stoneybrook" with this book feels like a "reunion with close friends."

"As we approach the 40-year anniversary of The Baby-Sitters Club, it’s deeply meaningful to know that the series has helped inspire a love of reading," Martin said. "I am humbled, honored, and incredibly grateful.”
Take a Sneak Peek at the 1st 'Baby-sitters Club' Graphic Novel
“The Baby-sitters Club Super Super Special: Baby-sitters on Board!” is the series' first super special graphic novel.

"The Baby-sitters Club boards the Ocean Princess for a cruise to the Bahamas and Disney World! Mary Anne meets a mysterious girl who never quite tells the truth. Claudia gets mixed up with a secret admirer. Kristy and Stacey make special friends, and Dawn has a fabulous romance," the official synopsis reads.

title: Why Amanda Batula, West Wilson’s Confirmed Romance Is Shocking Fans of ‘Summer House’
summary: Longtime "Summer House" co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson have confirmed they are dating after rampant speculation, fueling an already dramatic Season 10 release
story: Longtime "Summer House" co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson have confirmed they are dating after rampant speculation, fueling an already dramatic Season 10 release.
The new couple shocked fans after revealing their secret romance in a joint statement March 31 on their respective Instagram stories.
“We’ve seen the growing online speculation, so while this is still very new, we wanted to provide some clarity,” the statement, which Batula and Wilson individually posted, said.

“It was never our intention to purposely hide anything. Given the complicated relationship dynamics involved and the scrutiny that comes with being on a reality show, we needed a little space to process things privately before speaking on it," they continued.
Confirmation about the Bravo stars' romance comes amid weeks of online speculation about the nature of their relationship following Batula's split from estranged husband Kyle Cooke in January.
The rumors also hint of possible tension between Batula and close friend and co-star Ciara Miller, who had an on-and-off romance with Wilson for years.
Who Are West Wilson and Amanda Batula?

Batula and Wilson are both current stars of Bravo's "Summer House." Batula has appeared on the show since Season 1, and the show documented the beginning of her relationship with Cooke, whom she married in 2021.
Wilson joined the show in Season 8, which premiered in 2024.
Their joint statement March 31 confirming their romance comes on the heels of Batula’s split from Cooke, which they announced on Instagram in January.

“After much reflection, we have mutually and amicably decided to part ways as a couple,” Batula and Cooke wrote, per People. “We share this with a heavy heart and kindly ask for your grace and support while we focus on our personal growth and healing.”
Batula and Cooke's marital struggles have featured prominently on "Summer House," including a Season 3 confession from Cooke that he had cheated on Batula.

Meanwhile, Wilson has been involved for several years in an on-and-off dynamic with Batula’s friend Ciara Miller, another castmate. Batula expressed gratitude for Miller's support during her breakup during an interview with Marie Claire published in March 2026.

"It would have been a very difficult summer to get through without Ciara. She is one of the kindest, most loving, loyal friends I’ve ever had. She sends me texts out of the blue, reminding me how strong, powerful, smart, and kind I am — I don’t think she even realizes how much I need those texts," Batula said.

Miller and Wilson started dating while filming Season 8 in the summer of 2023 before breaking up that December. But the sparks never fully faded. In Season 10, which is currently airing and was filmed in the summer of 2025, Wilson and Miller continue to flirt, with the door seemingly open to a rekindling.
In Batula's March interview, she also revealed Wilson had also helped her during her separation from Cooke, saying Wilson was "supportive" in "ways (she) wasn’t even aware of when filming."
"It’s not performative; it’s just really authentic to who West is. He checks Kyle and puts him in his place, and you’ll continue to see him be there for me throughout the season," Batula said.
What Did Amanda Batula and West Wilson Say in the Joint Statement?
Batula and Wilson explained in their March 31 statement their “connection” developed from a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”
“We’ve shown up for each other as friends over the years, through all the highs and lows, and what’s developed recently was the last thing either of us expected,” they wrote.

“As our feelings evolved, we wanted to take time to understand exactly what we were feeling,” they added. “We also recognize that this has had an impact beyond just us and never wanted our actions to cause any hurt or be perceived as careless.”
Just days before releasing the statement, Wilson denied the rumors involving him and Batula on "Watch What Happens Live." He said they had "just been hanging out."
"If it's not clear, that's a very important person to me, and I care about her a lot," he said on March 24. "That's a friend. Hope it's clear."
Have Ciara Miller or Kyle Cooke Reacted?
TODAY.com has reached out to reps for Batula, Wilson, Miller and Cooke for comment.
While neither Miller nor Cooke has commented publicly on the confirmed "connection" between their exes, as of March 31, Miller does not follow Wilson and Batula on Instagram.

Miller's first Instagram post since the release of Wilson and Batula's statement was of her interviewing the stars of "Your Friends & Neighbors" on the red carpet of its Season 2 premiere on March 30.
In one clip from her interviews, Miller asked show star Jon Hamm how he's feeling.
"Wonderful, thank you, how are you?" Hamm said.
"I'm good. Well, I've been better," Miller responded.

Before their statement, Cooke was asked about the speculation regarding Wilson and Batula on "Watch What Happens Live" March 10. He called the rumors "outrageous" and said, "I don't think there's any merit to it."
Asked how he'd react if it was true, Cooke said, "It would certainly catch me by surprise and feel a little reckless, and I think I'd probably be the last person people would be worried about.
"I think Ciara would probably have something to say," he added. "But if it made Amanda happy, I think I would just have to vote yay."
What's Happening with 'Summer House'?
"Summer House" Season 10 is currently airing. New episodes air on Tuesdays at 8 p.m. Episode 9 aired on March 31.
After Wilson and Batula released their statement, Bravo's Andy Cohen confirmed the Season 10 cast reunion has not yet been filmed.
"My team is getting ready for THE SUMMER HOUSE REUNION! Send me all of your questions for the cast NOW and let me know where you’re from in the question!" he posted on X March 31.

title: Read With Jenna's April 2026 Is an 'Extraordinary Book' That Will 'Open Hearts and Change Minds'
summary: Read With Jenna's April 2026 pick is "Upward Bound" by Woody Brown.
story: Jenna Bush Hager says she was “so enthralled” by “Upward Bound,” her April 2026 book club pick, that she read it on a single airplane ride, “unable to tear myself away from the beauty in its pages.”
"Upward Bound" is Woody Brown's debut novel. Brown is autistic and nonverbal. With the help of his mom, Mary Brown, he uses a letter board to communicate and write.

"He has written an extraordinary story about what becomes possible when even one person believes in you. Set in an adult day care center called Upward Bound, we meet an unforgettable cast of characters — Carlos, Mariana, Jorge, Tom, Ann, and, at the heart of it all, Walter, who is only truly understood by his mother," Jenna says.
"This is a love story about mothers and sons — a window into a world so often misunderstood. I am confident it will open hearts and change minds," she continues.
Looking ahead to the release of his first novel, Brown, sitting next to his mother Mary Brown, tells TODAY.com he feels "nervous and excited."
"Upward Bound" by Woody Brown
"I knew that this story needed to be told, and thanks to my education and excellent mentors, I was in a uniquely apt position to tell it the right way," he says.
"How many like me are trapped in bodies, locked in silence, and yearning for education and true connection?"
In a taped interview with Jenna that aired on TODAY with Jenna and Sheinelle on March 31, Mary Brown said she was told her son was intellectually disabled and would never graduate high school.

Instead of accepting that, Mary Brown learned how to use a letter board. Together, they followed his high school curriculum through to his graduation in 2017. When she realized his next step was adult day care, Mary Brown quit her job so they could both attend UCLA.
"To finally be in the room where the learning was happening ... I felt like I was in heaven," Woody Brown told Jenna.
"We came to realize that he had real potential for a productive and happy life," Mary Brown said.
"Upward Bound" is the start, but not the end, of Woody Brown's writing ambitions — he's already on Chapter 6 of his next book.

Woody Brown tells TODAY.com he hopes readers take away two things from his debut: “First, that someone like me can be a good writer and want to read my next novel. Second, I hope that people will question their assumptions about the intelligence of profoundly disabled people,” he says.

His debut features an array of characters in the adult daycare center, including Tom, who has both cerebral palsy and a crush on the lifeguard, and Carlos, the employee who has a tender connection with Jorge, a nonspeaking client with a penchant for running away.
The book sometimes veers into dark places. "The tragedy is real," Woody Brown says. "So not to be honest about it would be unjust to the characters."
He also has a message for his younger self. "Your instincts are correct. You just have to wait for everybody else to catch up. Just keep learning, because a writer needs a good education," he says.

title: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page Are Living La Bella Vita on the Cover of Essence
summary: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page are the cover stars for Essence's latest edition. The two star in the upcoming rom-com "You, Me & Tuscany."
story: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page are showing off all their best angles as the cover stars of Essence's latest digital edition.
TODAY.com is exclusively revealing the actors’ stunning cover and photo spread ahead of the release of their upcoming rom-com, “You, Me & Tuscany.”
In the photos, the two look quite dashing while posing amid the Italian architecture. Naturally, a red moped made an appearance, too.

Out April 10, “You, Me & Tuscany” follows Anna (Bailey), a young woman who is down on her luck after losing her job. She meets Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor), a handsome Italian man who happens to own a villa that sits empty in Tuscany.

Inspired and with no place to call home, Anna decides to go to Matteo’s villa without him knowing. There, she becomes entangled in a web of lies and meets Matteo’s swoon-worthy cousin Michael (Page). As feelings grow between the two, the lies Anna has told could negatively impact their future.

“I am in my very grown woman era,” Bailey tells Essence in the accompanying cover story. “This is the first project that I am showing myself in my full adulthood. And I loved it because it reflects the times that I’m in right now.”
Not only is this a stepping stone for the singer-turned-actor after starring in “The Little Mermaid” and “The Color Purple,” but it’s also a project that made her proud to celebrate Black love.

“It was so important for me to be a part of a project where I could see myself on-screen, where I could see Black love on-screen,” Bailey says. “A film where I can see us figuring it out in a joyful way.”
Produced by Will Packer, the man behind films like “Think Like a Man” and “Girls Trip,” he found the perfect stars and helped bring the joyful love to life alongside director Kat Coiro.

“And that’s very much the journey the characters are on,” he says. “Letting your co-star unlock the pieces of you that can trust someone else, and that can lean into the pieces of you that you’re holding back.”

He adds, “This movie is very much about learning to trust each other. When you’re doing romantic stories, it’s about building an emotional trust with each other, knowing that you can go to places where you are expressing very, very vulnerable things.”

title: The Kelces Just Got Their 5th Girl, and She’s a Dog! What They Named Her
summary: The Kelce family has added another girl to the mix … an adorable new puppy named Bernadette.
story: The Kelce family has added another girl to the mix … an adorable new puppy.

Kylie Kelce revealed in the latest episode of her “FAFO” series, an extension of her “Not Gonna Lie” podcast, that six weeks ago she worked with the Pennsylvania Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals’ Main Line Animal Rescue to find a four-legged friend for their household.

“Jason (Kelce) does know that I’m here today,” Kylie Kelce said at the start of the video. “We did discuss whether or not he would be peeved if I came home with an animal. And when I said rate on a scale of one to 10, he said, ‘10.’”
But, Kylie Kelce cheekily added, her husband didn’t clarify “which side of the scale was pissed.”
Throughout the episode, the mother of four met multiple friendly dogs and cats while also learning about PSPCA’s rescue efforts and how the organization cares for animals.

Toward the end of the episode, Kylie Kelce sat in a dog pen surrounded by eight-week-old German shepherd puppies. She mused over which one had a personality that would be the perfect fit for the Kelces.
She also already had a name picked out: Bernadette.
“Are you a Bernadette? Tell me the truth,” she said to one puppy. She then turned to the camera and said, “Look at her little dimple where her little whiskers are."
She knew that the puppy in her arms was the one she wanted to adopt.

“I can’t help myself! Someone let me loose in a rescue and this is what happened,” Kylie Kelce said. “I met some very cute cats. But, like I’ve said a million times at this point, we’re not ready for that. When I am ready for that, I’m going to march my a-- right back to the PSPCA and I’m going to find me a cat that wants to come home and live at the Kelce household.”
She continued, “But for now … may I introduce Bernadette Kelce!”
Kylie Kelce officially welcomed Bernadette to the family before predicting that her husband might have a different opinion on her name.
“Thanks again to the real ones for tuning in,” she signed off before including pictures of some of the animals featured in the episode that have already been adopted.
Fans also gave Bernadette a warm welcome in the comments.
“Bernadette just won the lottery for sure!” one said. “Not gonna lie… I seriously thought she was going home with two for a minute there.  LOL. Congrats to the Kelce family on their newest arrival.”
Another wrote, “Oh Bernadette, wait until you find out what it’s like living with the Kelce’s. What a life!!!”
A third suggested, “We for sure need the follow up video showing Jason and the girls reacting to a new puppy!!!!”

title: What Happened to Lamar Odom? New Details Emerge About His 2015 Overdose in Netflix's 'Untold'
summary: The new Netflix documentary, “Untold: The Death & Life of Lamar Odom,” chronicles the NBA star’s addiction history and what happened the night he overdosed.
story: The new Netflix documentary, “Untold: The Death & Life of Lamar Odom,” chronicles the NBA star’s history.
The two-time NBA champion has endured a public battle with addiction, including a well-known 2015 incident in which he was found unconscious in a brothel.
“This documentary traces the life of Lamar Odom, from his rise to NBA fame and marriage to Khloé Kardashian to his near-fatal overdose in a Nevada brothel,” Netflix said.
In the documentary, Odom, 46, examines the overdose, his future and his marriage to Kardashian. The two tied the knot in 2009 and were married until their divorce in 2016.
What Happened to Lamar Odom in 2015? The Doc Shares New Details

Odom, who played in the NBA for 14 seasons from 1999 until 2013, was hospitalized in 2015 after he was found unconscious following an overdose while at a brothel in Crystal, Nevada. He suffered kidney failure, multiple heart attacks and strokes. (Odom has specified that he had six heart attacks and 12 strokes.)
He was placed in a coma and on life support before he became conscious again three days later.

Kardashian had signed divorce papers prior to his overdose, but the split had not yet been finalized. Kardashian, who remained by his side after the medical episode, said she had a sort of premonition that something bad would happen to him.
“I was doing a photo shoot for People magazine, and the night before, I had a dream about Lamar, that he was dead, and I was at his funeral, and I was sobbing,” Kardashian said in the documentary.
She says her sister, Kim Kardashian, was the one to let her know what had happened to him.
“In my interview, Kim called me three times,” she said. “I get in the car, I call her, and she’s like, ‘Lamar overdosed. He’s in Vegas.’ And I just remember screaming.”

The incident was preceded by years of drug use, Odom said in the documentary. Kardashian said she was aware of his drug use and worked to keep it a secret from the public. “I was such an enabler without knowing I was an enabler. But I just felt such a responsibility to cover this up, hold it together and protect him,” she said.
Khloe Kardashian said Odom’s late father, Joe Odom, wanted to take his son off of life support.

“I remember walking in around that time, and I kid you not, it was a scene out of a movie, and I’m like, ‘Sorry, Joe, I’m still his wife. What do you need?’ And Joe wanted a pair of Nikes and $100 at a hotel room for the night. And Joe left and never came back,” she recalled.

“This is my husband. Through sickness and health, I have to be there for him. I have to show up for him, and I was going to do everything I could to make sure that he had the best fighting chance. And I just trusted myself more than I trusted anyone in his circle.”
Odom’s children, son LJ and daughter Destiny, also were interviewed in the documentary and said they visited the hospital with the understanding that they would be saying goodbye to their father.
“When we went into the hospital room, it was hard seeing him all tubed up and everything,” Destiny said. “He was so swollen. I remember how swollen he was.”
“I was like, ‘You can’t leave. Like we still have memories to make,'" Destiny continued.
What Does Lamar Odom Say About the Incident in the Documentary?
Odom spoke about waking up after three days of a coma, “ripping tubes out of my neck.”
“I still got the scar from it,” he said.
Kardashian said he looked up at her, said, “Hey, baby,” and fell back asleep.
Odom spoke about the difficulty of the recovery process, calling it humiliating. “I won two championships. I’m Lamar Odom. I can’t walk, can’t talk,” he said.

Kardashian spent four months at the hospital by his side. “We were determined to get him walking and moving again,” she said over footage of his recovery process. He eventually learned how to walk again.
Odom acknowledged the matter weighed heavily on Kardashian.
“It probably was harder for her, because I couldn’t say thank you, or I appreciate you. Or couldn’t show any — I wouldn’t even cry,” he said.
“It’s like I was a heartbeat,” he said.
How the 2015 Incident Playing Into His Divorce From Kardashian
“Untold” shows how Odom’s drug use and the 2015 incident led to his divorce from Kardashian.

In the leadup to an intervention about Odom’s drug use, Kardashian said she was encouraged to offer up divorce as an ultimatum. She did, actually not believing he would take her up on it. To her surprise, he did.

“Everyone around me was like, ‘He’ll never say he wants the divorce.’ We do the intervention. I give my speech. And he was like, ‘OK, all I want is my passport, and yeah, let’s get the … divorce,’ ” she said, adding that she “didn’t want” a divorce.
After caring for Odom at the hospital, she rented a house for Odom to continue his recovery. However, when she caught him using drugs again, she ended the relationship.
“He was playing me so I can continue this lifestyle for him. I said, ‘By Monday, you need out of this house. I’m done. I’m not paying for a thing, and I never want to speak to you again,’” she said.
They had a tense reunion in the Season 6 premiere of “The Kardashians,” speaking for the first time in nine years.
“I took you through a lot, huh?” he said.
“I can’t say that was the easiest time,” she responded.
“No, not for me either,” he said. “Yeah, I f--d up ... with you. I love you for trying your hardest with me, man.”
Where Is Lamar Odom Now?
Odom, at the end of the documentary, speaks about his future plans.

“Before I die I promise I’m going to learn how to swim and go back to school because I got a chance right now to coach in college really soon, and probably after that, probably in the NBA, if I want to,” he said.
“But I really want to use coaching college to go back to school. My grandma went back to school. She was 50 years old before graduating.”

Earlier this year, Odom was arrested in Las Vegas for driving under the influence. A lawyer entered a not guilty plea in March on Odom’s behalf. Odom’s manager, Gina Rodriguez, told People he checked into rehab to address marijuana use. TODAY.com can confirm the documentary completed filming before Odom’s 2026 arrest.
Odom spoke with USA Today ahead of the documentary’s release and said he was 60 days sober.
He likened revisiting his past for the documentary to "swimming in a cesspool of trauma."

title: Jenna Bush Hager's Signature Dance Move, 'The Bucking Bronco,' Leaves Olivia Munn Speechless
summary: Olivia Munn wasn't quite sure what to make of one of Jenna Bush Hager's signature dance moves while appearing on TODAY March 31.
story: Olivia Munn isn't quite sure what to make of one of Jenna Bush Hager's signature dance moves.
While appearing on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on March 31, the actor weighed in on some hot topics with Jenna and her guest co-host, Willie Geist.

The trio discussed a new debate that's pitting millennials versus Gen Z, which suggests that members of the younger generation aren't exactly enthused when they hit the dance floor, while millennials give it their all.
"I mean, I loved going to dances when I was younger and just getting out there and dancing," Munn said.
The actor went on to say she likes to request Dr. Dre's song "Nuthin’ but a 'G' Thang" at weddings and exhibited her dance moves.
"He's seen one of my signature moves, which I didn't think I was gonna bring out," Jenna said before revealing its name: the Bucking Bronco.
Munn didn't seem to be familiar with the move, so Jenna explained it.
"I ride somebody across the floor," Jenna said.
"You're lying," Munn said in disbelief.
"She does," Willie confirmed.
Munn was curious to know what the move looked like but clarified that she wasn't going to make Jenna do it on air.
"It's not Willie, but it was our friend Emily. We'll go ahead and drop her name. She won't mind. And I was on top of her," Jenna began.
The move is performed by one person crawling on the floor on all fours as Jenna sits on their back and swings her arm in the air in a circle — much like a cowboy might on a wild horse.
“It’s not really a dance move so much,” Willie said.
Munn was flabbergasted.
"Jenna, I've never been speechless with you," she said.
"I don't know what to say," Munn added.
"I'm not sure what you should say," Jenna joked.
This is hardly the first time Jenna has mentioned her signature move. In 2018, she displayed it on air while chatting with Carson Daly.
"It's not appropriate, I don't think for television. Well, actually, come here, come on Donna. It's appropriate for Donna," she said as her colleague Donna Farizan appeared.
That time Jenna played the role of the "horse" and asked Donna to sit on her back as she exhibited the move.
"Oh my God, this is like a Tuesday gone wild," Donna said.
"That's my dance move I do at weddings," Jenna said.
"What's happening here?" Carson joked.

title: The 'Age of Attraction' Reunion Isn't on Netflix. Here's How to Watch
summary: The Age of Attraction Reunion Isn't on Netflix. Here's how to watch the Age of Attraction reunion on the Viall Files on YouTube.
story: Netflix is taking a new approach to the "Age of Attraction" reunion.

The reality dating series followed singles as they searched for "the one" without asking each other their ages. Once two singles decided to commit to each other, they could reveal their ages. From there, they could choose to move in together and continue the experiment.
In the Season 1 finale, four couples stayed together, despite significant age gaps, and one couple went their separate ways.
During the reunion special, viewers will learn which couples are still together.
Below, we're sharing what you need to know before tuning in.
What Time Does the ‘Age of Attraction' Reunion Come Out?
The Season 1 “Age of Attraction” reunion will premiere on Wednesday, April 1 at 4 p.m. PT / 7 p.m. ET.
Where Is the ‘Age of Attraction' Reunion Streaming? (It's Not Netflix!)
The "Age of Attraction" Season 1 reunion will not debut on Netflix. Instead, it will air as an episode of "The Viall Files," the podcast of “Age of Attraction” host Nick Viall.
Viall’s wife and “Age of Attraction” co-host Natalie Joy will also appear in the reunion special, too.
The reunion will premiere on Viall's YouTube channel. It's also available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or wherever you listen to podcast.
Which 'Age of Attraction' Stars Will Appear in the Reunion?

Season 1 of "Age of Attraction" followed six couples who committed to each other early on in the season. Vanelle Fenmou and Jorge Sanchez broke up before decision day, but they will join the other five couples during the reunion special.
A trailer for the reunion implies that Pfeiffer Hill, who decided to stay with Derrick Fleming in the season finale, was not present for the reunion.
Here’s a list of the cast members who appear in the reunion:

title: 'Boy Meets World' Star William Daniels Celebrates 99th Birthday With Family
summary: Former "Boy Meets World" star William Daniels is celebrating his 99th birthday
story: Former "Boy Meets World" star William Daniels is celebrating his 99th birthday.

The Los Angeles-based Daniels, who portrayed beloved teacher and principal George Feeny on the 1990s sitcom "Boy Meets World," tells TODAY.com he’s ringing in his birthday on March 31 with his wife, Bonnie, 96, their two sons, Michael, 62, and Robert, 60, and their families.

"I'm going to spend my birthday with my family, and Michael is going to feed us," says Daniels, whose acting career spans decades and includes his Emmy-winning performance as Dr. Mark Craig on "St. Elsewhere" and voicing KITT, the state-of-the-art car driven by David Hasselhoff on "Knight Rider."
Just as the wise Mr. Feeny doled out life advice to his students, the affable Daniels opens up about what he’s done to stay healthy and fit over the years.
Stay Grateful
At the top of the list? A sense of gratitude, he says.

"I think the most important thing you should know is that I am very lucky. I've been able to work and keep active and have a sense of purpose," says Daniels. "I was on 'Boy Meets World' until I was 73 years old!"
Daniels says the greatest source of joy in his life has been his family, including Bonnie, whom he married in 1951.
"I have a wonderful wife, Bonnie, who has put up with me for 75 years," he jokes. "She keeps me busy."
Staying in touch with fans is also a treat for the actor, who appeared at both the San Diego Rocket Con 2026 and Pasadena Comic Con 2026 this year. He can also be found on Cameo.
Exercise Regularly
As for exercise, Daniels says he swam and played tennis for many years. These days, he and Bonnie work out with a personal trainer twice a week.
"My advice to others is to keep moving," says the actor. "Do things you enjoy and find some kind of exercise that you like."
A Balanced Diet
Daniels says he's looking forward to his birthday dinner. Michael will whip up a homemade meal of his father's favorite food: pasta.
There will also be a fresh salad, which is Bonnie's favorite.
Daniels says he doesn't adhere to any special diet, and he certainly doesn't shy away from comfort foods.

He and Bonnie regularly go to their favorite local deli for a hearty breakfast. Daniels' typical order? "Poached eggs, toast, bacon, and a side of some fruit. Oh, and a small orange juice and coffee," he says.
He also stays hydrated by drinking a healthy amount of water. "It's important," he says.
Keep Your Mind Sharp
Daniels also knows the importance of keeping his mind sharp and alert, so he makes it a point to read daily and keep up with current events.
"I read The New York Times. That takes me the whole day," he says, laughing.

title: Barbie Superfans Are Calling This 3-Day Event the New Fyre Fest: 'It Was Rough'
summary: People who went to the Barbie Dream Fest tell TODAY.com what the experience was like.
story: The Barbie Dream Fest wasn’t exactly a dream for many attendees. The event, which took place in Fort Lauderdale, Florida between March 27 to 29, was advertised as “three days of glam, nostalgia, and dream-big energy” for Barbie fans of all ages.
But many attendees have taken to social media to express their disappointment in the event’s execution, prompting organizers to issue full refunds to ticketholders.
What happened at the event and what do attendees have to say about their experience? We’re sharing everything you need to know below, including Mattel’s response.
What Was Barbie Dream Fest Advertised As?
Barbie Dream Fest was described as “The Ultimate Barbie Fan Event” on its official Instagram page.

A website for Barbie Dream Fest outlined the schedule for the three-day event. The packed schedule included activities like “dream flow yoga,” a guided painting class and talks with Barbie designers and public figures like WNBA star Angel Reese and “Love on the Spectrum” star Madison Marilla. There was also a photo opp with Serena Williams.
Ticket prices ranged from $30 day passes for kids and $69 for adults, through to an "Icon Pass" that was over $500.
What Barbie Dream Fest Attendees Had to Say

The event wasn’t what many hoped for. Online, people are sharing imagery from within the Barbie Dream Fest, including festival-goers traversing large swaths of concrete flooring and showcasing small attractions.
One TikToker called it a "scam." A Reddit user suggested that the Barbie Dream Fest was a case of false advertising. “Anyone else who went to Dreamfest….. we got Fyre-fested.”
The user was one of many to invoke Fyre Fest, the notorious 2017 fiasco that has become shorthand for in-person experiences that don’t measure up to expectations.

Speaking to TODAY.com, Mary Anne Trotter, a mom of five and a "hard-core" Barbie collector, flew in from Toronto. She took time off from work, where she has a Barbie-themed office, and had been looking forward to the event for months.

"I bought the ticket the minute they went on sale and bought the dream pass. I thought it was going be the most amazing Barbie thing I’ve been to. I was so disappointed. The way they advertised this was so not true. It was the worst experience,” she says. "It’s sad because I love Barbie."
Attendee Jessica Nova tells TODAY.com the event’s execution was “embarrassing.”
“When they said ‘life-size interactive Barbie house,’ we definitely weren’t expecting a cardboard backdrop,” they say.

As another example, Nova says says certain ticket tiers were promised a swag bag. Nova was disappointed to only receive a small hand sanitizer. When asked where actual swag bag was, staff members said they were delayed.
Nova says the ticket level above the package they purchased was going to get hand sanitizer ... and a hairbrush.

Attendee Cherry Bonbon, who spoke to TODAY.com using her stage name, tells TODAY.com she was initially taken aback by the event’s low attendance. As she made her way around the convention center, she was surprised that there weren’t many vendors on site and says some of them were “not at all related to Barbie or Barbie adjacent.” For instance, one vendor was advertising windows.
“It was rough,” she says.
On the second day of the event, Bonbon was pleased to see that event organizers did “enlarge the roller rink” and added a disco ball.

“But the roller rink was supposed to be an '‘80s neon disco roller rink.' It was a roller rink. None of the other words applied. There was no music to make it ‘80s. There was a disco ball, but it wasn’t moving. There were no lights reflecting off of it,” she says.

Speaking to TODAY.com, TikTok user @SailorTortilla expected “immersive, adult-sized photo opps” or at the very least, “pink carpet throughout the venue instead of concrete.” Instead, she says she was met with an event geared toward children.
“The marketing featured adults in the photography, which set the expectation that the event would be more adult and collector-focused,” she says.
She managed to have a good time with her friends, but says the event “felt like a letdown given the price.”

“The execution didn’t match the expectations set by the marketing. I’ve been to better conventions organized by college clubs. You don’t need to have a giant convention space to create excitement and a special themed atmosphere,” she says.

Julia Borga, meanwhile, says she had a positive experience at the Barbie Dream Fest. Going in, her expectations were “higher” than what she experienced, but she enjoyed it all the same, especially the photo booth and crafts.
“I was like, ‘Well, we’re here and let’s still have fun with what they have,” she says.

“Yes, it was a letdown, but I think (people should) just remember to see the positive in anything, really. You can still have fun with little things. I mean, kids did. It was the parents that were let down,” she says.
What Has Mattel Said?
A Mattel spokesperson tells TODAY.com the event was created by Mischief Management, a company that licensed the Barbie brand from Mattel.

“We are working with Mischief Management, who are managing attendee feedback and issuing full refunds to everyone who purchased tickets. We want every fan experience to be an excellent one,” the spokesperson shared in a statement.
A Mischief Management spokesperson tells TODAY.com the event organizers are “incredibly grateful” for attendees and they “appreciate the passion and engagement from the Barbie community.”

“Bringing fans together — alongside Barbie role models, designers, partners, and global icons who embody the true spirit of Barbie — was at the heart of this event. Barbie Dream Fest was created as an intimate fan convention designed to foster meaningful connection, inspiration, and closer access to Barbie and her world. We will be providing full refunds to everyone who purchased tickets,” the spokesperson said in a statement.

title: See Early Look at ‘Masters of the Universe’ Trailer
summary: "Masters of the Universe" stars Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba and Camila Mendes introduced a trailer for the movie on TODAY.
story: TODAY got a “master”-ful glimpse at what could be one of this summer’s blockbuster movies.
“Masters of the Universe” stars Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba and Camila Mendes personally shared the trailer with TODAY for the live-action flick in a taped segment that aired March 31.
“Hey, PopStart crew, I’m Nicholas Galitzine,” the actor begins before Elba and Mendes introduce themselves.
“‘Masters of the Universe’ is an epic adventure filled with creatures, heroes and villains and, most importantly, exhilarating fun,” he adds.
“We are so excited to give the TODAY show an exclusive look at the official trailer for the film today,” Mendes adds.

Galitzine plays the main character, He-Man, whose regular identity is Prince Adam of Eternia. Elba plays Man-At-Arms, also known as Duncan, while Mendes portrays Teela. The film also stars Jared Leto, who plays the villainous Skeletor, as well as Kristen Wiig, Morena Baccarin, Alison Brie and James Purefoy.
In the film, Galitzine's character was raised on Earth to escape war.
"Everything changed since you left," Mendes' Teela tells Adam as he sees his home planet decimated.
The trailer also previews Adam returning to his ancestor's Power Sword and becoming He-Man, the most powerful man in the universe, as well as his epic showdown with Skeletor.

“Masters of the Universe” is due in theaters June 5. It follows the first live action attempt to bring the He-Man character to life on the big screen, in a 1987 movie of the same name that starred Dolph Lundgren, Courteney Cox and Frank Langella.

title: Céline Dion Announces Comeback with Paris Concerts
summary: Céline Dion has announced her grand return to the stage with a series of concerts in Paris. See the dates.
story: Céline Dion has announced her grand return to the stage with a series of concerts in Paris.

On March 30, the music icon, who took a break from performing after publicly revealing her stiff person syndrome diagnosis, celebrated her 58th birthday by announcing that her comeback will kick off this fall.
“Hi, everyone. You know, in my career I’ve recorded a lot of birthday messages, but this is the first time I’ve ever recorded one for my own birthday,” Dion said in a video posted on Instagram.
She shared that she has been in good spirits as she manages her health. She also revealed that she has started singing and dancing again.

“But I have to tell you something very important,” she continued. “Over these last few years, every day that’s come by I’ve felt your prayers and support, with kindness and love. You were there for me. You’ve helped me in ways that I can’t even describe, and I’m truly so fortunate to have your support.”
Dion said she has been missing her fans. So, on her birthday, she wanted to give herself, and them, a special present.
“This year I’m getting the best gift of my life. I’m getting the chance to see you, to perform for you once again, in Paris, beginning in September this year,” she announced.
Dion smiled wide as she continued to speak about how excited she is to take the stage again.
I’m so ready to do this. I’m feeling good. I’m strong. I’m feeling excited. Obviously, of course, a little nervous,” she added.
At the end of her message, she told her fans, “But most of all, I am grateful to all of you. I can’t wait to see you again.”
In the caption, Dion said the presale for her Paris shows begins on April 7. Fans can register for access from March 30 to April 2 at CelineDion.com.
Fans celebrated her return in the comments.
“We love you Celine! Cant wait to see you shine on stage,” one said.

Another wrote, “It’s indescribable to see you so well, Celine! Words couldn’t express how happy and excited we are to see you performing again, the one thing you worked so hard for as we know how much the stage means to you! Happiest of birthdays and cannot wait to see you on stage!”
“Sheeeeeee’s back,” a third said and included multiple heart-eye emoji. “You look so good queen.”

Dion had to step away from performing for several years after being diagnosed with stiff person syndrome, which is a rare and serious neurological condition. Stiff person syndrome causes muscle stiffness in the torso and limbs, thus impacting her ability to sing.
It can also lead to heightened sensitivity to noise, touch and emotional stress that set off painful muscle spasms, according to the National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke.
The condition, with features of an autoimmune disease, affects about one or two people in a million, according to Johns Hopkins Medicine.
Although there is no cure for the condition, Yale Medicine reports that the symptoms can be controlled using medication, as well as physical occupational and aqua therapy.
Dion shared her diagnosis with her fans in 2022, and she canceled all of her upcoming concerts that were part of her world tour a few months later.
Nearly two years later, she sang for the first time since announcing her diagnosis at the Paris Olympics 2024 opening ceremony. She performed Edith Piaf’s “Hymne à l’amour.”
Soon, she will return to the City of Light and reunite with her fans. See the list of her 10 Paris concert dates at the Paris La Défense Arena, below.

title: 'Paradise' Season 3: Everything the Creators and Stars Have Revealed So Far
summary: Will Paradise have a Season 3? What will happen in Paradise Season 3 based on the Season 2 finale? What have they shared so far?
story: The Season 2 finale of Hulu’s “Paradise” delivered death, danger and a host of questions following a rollercoaster final episode.

The latest season of the post-apocalyptic thriller, which stars Sterling K. Brown as U.S. Secret Service agent Xavier Collins living in a city in an underground bunker in Colorado, ended with a stunning death and the reveal of a shadowy character who is part human, part machine.
Multiple characters don’t make it out of the episode alive, and the existence of Paradise itself is in jeopardy.
The question now is what the future holds for Xavier, the rest of the inhabitants of Paradise and the cryptic figure known as Alex. Will we get a third season of the show to find out?
Will There Be a Season 3 of “Paradise”?
Yes, “Paradise” will return for another season. Brown announced on the show’s Instagram on March 17 that it had been renewed for  a third go round.
He is shown in a video going through messages written on cue cards.
“Knock, knock. Paradise is full of secrets,” the cards read. “So many secrets that they can’t be contained to just 2 seasons.”
Show star Nicole Brydon Bloom spoke on TODAY on March 21 about the show getting picked up for a third season.

“It was so exciting. There were whispers that it would happen, but it’s always so thrilling to see that people are enjoying the show  and that Hulu wants to do a third season,” she told Peter Alexander and Laura Jarrett.
Brown told TODAY.com in 2025 that show creator Dan Fogelman (“This Is Us” and “Crazy, Stupid, Love”) envisioned a story arc that would run across three seasons.
“Fogelman is the secret sauce behind the whole thing,” Brown told Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones on TODAY March 23.
Brown likened it to the progress of the iconic HBO drama “The Wire,” where “each season sort of explores a different aspect of a community.”
The “This Is Us” star said each season of “Paradise” might examine “the same community, but from a slightly different aspect.”

“For us, we’re looking at what happens to the world inside of Paradise? The people who prepared for it, who knew that it was coming, how do they manage the situation? How do people manage the situation they had no idea was coming and are forced to live without any sort of infrastructure?” Brown said.

title: The Pussycat Dolls Are Back. But Their Music Never Left
summary: The Pussycat Dolls are back with a brand new song and a reunion tour, but a familiar message.“It is incredible to have the opportunity to perform these songs in 2026.
story: The Pussycat Dolls are back with a brand new song and a reunion tour, but a familiar message.

“It is incredible to have the opportunity to perform these songs in 2026. Women in this world need a space to feel free, to feel strong, to feel like they can own their space,” Kimberly Wyatt tells TODAY.com. “And to be able to bring that to the stage and create an opportunity for everybody that comes to the show to be free with us is just amazing.”
In 2019, the Pussycat Dolls, a girl band that rose to fame in the 2000s with hits like “Don’t Cha” and “When I Grow Up,” attempted a reunion in 2019, having disbanded back in 2010.

Nicole Scherzinger, Carmit Bachar, Ashley Roberts, Jessica Sutta and Kimberly Wyatt reunited for a performance during the finale of “The X Factor: Celebrity.” They released a new song, “React,” featuring their signature dance pop girl band sound, and announced plans for a reunion tour, which was meant to start in April 2020. The COVID-19 pandemic and a legal dispute between Scherzinger and band founder Robin Antin effectively canceled those plans.

Fast forward to March 12, 2026, and the trio of Scherzinger, Roberts and Wyatt announced the release of their new track, “Club Song,” and an upcoming 53-date reunion tour called the “PCD Forever Tour.”
Previewing the tour, which is scheduled to kick off June 5 in Palm Desert, California, Wyatt says the trio is excited to bring the individual experiences they’ve had to the stage.

“To bring that into the Pussycat Dolls and see what it looks like in 2026, it feels really progressive, and what that will feel like with the music and the choreography and sort of the story of the Pussycat Dolls on tour is going to be phenomenal,” Wyatt says.
The tour is also an opportunity to “reintroduce ourselves to this younger generation,” Roberts says.
“What’s exciting is that it’s nostalgic,” she says. “We’ve got our fans that have grown up with us and know our songs, and then they’re maybe bringing their kiddos to the concert this time.”
“Also, I mean, I go on TikTok, and our songs are still trending, and kids are doing different combinations and choreography to it,” she adds.
But while younger generations may know the music, they might not know PCD.
“You always say, ‘They gonna learn today,’” Scherzinger says.
“And also we’re going to get them excited because we’re going to throw down,” she adds.
“And stretch before we throw down,” Roberts chimes in.

What unites their songs, including “Club Song,” are themes of female independence, friendship and confidence. Wyatt says she believes the Pussycat Dolls can teach the next generation “the power of self.”
“It lives in the Pussycat Dolls’ music, creating that space, like ‘Club Song’ says, so you can just go out there and feel it is just everything,” Wyatt adds.
As for any plans to release more songs?

“We’re always working on music,” Scherzinger says. “But right now, we’re really excited. First of all, ‘React’ didn’t have its moment, so we’re excited. I don’t know, is there going to be water? Are there going to be chairs?

“We’re really excited about ‘Club Song,’” she adds. “We’re super proud of that. It’s such a different industry now, and when we’ve got other songs, well, you just gotta pay attention, because we’re full of surprises.”

title: Jon Hamm and His Wife Are Rewatching ‘Mad Men’ For the 1st Time. He Reviews His Performance
summary: Jon Hamm says he and his wife are rewatching "Mad Men" for the first time since it signed off in 2015.
story: Jon Hamm is looking back on his signature role.
Hamm, who can currently be seen in Apple TV’s “Your Friends & Neighbors,” says he is in the middle of rewatching “Mad Men.”
“My wife and I have been rewatching ‘Mad Men,’ because I haven’t done it. I’ve stayed away,” he said March 30 on TODAY.
“I’ve only seen the show when it first aired, and it went off the air in 2015, so it’s been 11 years since that.”

“Mad Men” ran for seven seasons from 2007 until 2015, winning a total of 16 Emmy Awards, including four for outstanding drama series. Hamm’s wife, Anna Osceola, also appeared on the show, playing Clementine in the series finale.
Hamm, who won an Emmy in 2015 for his portrayal of Don Draper on the AMC drama, says he’s enjoyed looking back on his work.
“It’s been a fun ride to look back. Obviously, it’s hard to look at yourself skinnier and with more hair and all the other things. But, I don’t know, I’m doing OK. I guess I’m doing OK,” he said.
Hamm said now seems to be the ideal time to go back and watch the show again.

“It was just a fun exercise,” he said. “And I had been doing a lot of press and talking about it. I was like, ‘I should probably familiarize myself with it a little more.’ And it’s been fun so far. We’re about two thirds away through season two. So we got a lot to go to, too.”
As far as his performance, Hamm is pleased with what he sees.
“I’m liking it," he said with a laugh. "This kid’s got a future."

title: 'Paradise' Season 2 Finale Delivers Death, Danger and a New Twist. Here's What Happens in the End
summary: What happens at the end of Paradise Season 2? Here is the recap of Paradise Season 2 Episode 8.
story: Spoilers ahead for the “Paradise” Season 2 finale.
The “Paradise” Season 2 finale is an emotional rollercoaster that will leave you wanting to know what happens next.
The season begins with Sterling K. Brown's character Xavier’s search for his wife, Teri Enuka Okuma), outside of the bunker.

New characters and even more twists and turns are introduced along the way, showing how communities survived and rebuilt after the catastrophic events in Season 1. Strangers turn into friends, friends turn into enemies and some enemies have changes of heart. Many lives are lost — some intentional, some not.
What went down in the Season 2 finale of “Paradise”? Read below to unpack that wild and unexpected ending.
What Happens In the 'Paradise' Season 2 Finale?

The eighth episode titled “Exodus” begins nine years earlier at Caltech with Link (Thomas Doherty), also known as Dylan, showing professor Henry Miller (Patrick Fischler) his new invention that can “predict anything that can go wrong in the future in microseconds.”

“This is the first AI-controlled quantum computer that’s ever been built,” Dylan tells Henry, who teaches advanced wave functions, superposition and quantum entanglement. This is how their relationship begins.

In Episode 7, Link arrives at the bunker and meets Sinatra (Julianne Nicholson) face-to-face. During their meeting, he asks where Alex is but she won't budge. When it’s time to go, one of Link’s crew members calls him “Dylan,” which elicits a reaction from Sinatra. She asks him his date of birth; May 16, he says. But more on the significance of that later.
At the end of the seventh episode, Sinatra says, “Hi, Alex” to someone ... or is it something?
This is where things start to get connected and intertwined.
Who Is Alex in 'Paradise'?
Alex (Gwen Holloway) is Henry's wife and is suffering from Huntington’s disease, which causes nerve cells in the brain to decay over time.

As the episode continues, it turns out that Alex is both a human and a machine. In flashback scenes, Henry meets with Sinatra and her science advisor, Dr. Chase, to help her create “the fastest computer possible” with the mission to solve the climate crisis before it becomes a catastrophe.
“Alex will perform,” Henry says, sharing that he named the AI system after his wife.

Turns out Henry and Dylan did create the fastest computer ever ... and it scared them. At one point, Henry tells Sinatra that he needs to shut down the machine because it was starting to get dangerous and “trying to manipulate time.” It also started to predict the future.
But first, back to why Sinatra was shocked to learn that Link's name is really Dylan.
The Complicated Relationship Between Dylan and Sinatra

Viewers recall that Sinatra hired now-deceased Agent Billy (Jon Beavers) to kill Henry. Dylan came face-to-face with Billy when he walked into Henry and Alex’s house and found them dead. Because of their work on the computer, Dylan knew who Sinatra was and all about the plans for the underground bunker. It’s assumed that Sinatra killed Henry so that she could keep AI “Alex” and not destroy it. This is also why Dylan wants to know where “Alex” is.
Sinatra discovering that Link's real name is Dylan is important because that is the name of her son who died. His May 16 birthdate is also significant because it is when her late son was born.
In the finale, Sinatra says that Dylan is her son — or at least believes it. When she’s in front of “Alex,” she tells Dr. Chase that she thinks she saw one of the computer’s “anomalies.”
“My son, Dylan,” she says, to which he replies, “That’s unlikely.”
She says she “can’t explain it” and knows that it doesn’t make sense, but she is certain Dylan is her son.
'Alex' Makes a Major Prediction That Changes the Course of Many Lives
Dr. Chase then tells Sinatra that “Alex” has started to communicate and make predictions.

“And what she’s predicted has come true,” he says, adding that she predicted that Sinatra would arrive there today. He hands her a message created by “Alex,” which includes dates, possible coordinates and a “user” who is meant to “activate it.”
But there is also another message “Alex” has communicated: Sinatra will be dead by the end of the day.
Xavier and Teri Return to the Bunker, Chaos Ensues

Xavier (Sterling K. Brown) and Teri (Enuka Okuma) are seen on the train making their way back to the bunker. Both asleep, Xavier appears to have a dream in which Dylan walks in front of him and tells him “we have to move fast.” However, he’s never met Dylan. He only has the photo ID that Annie had given him after she died giving birth to her and Dylan’s baby.

Inside Paradise, oxygen failure is happening but the doors are in full lockdown mode to keep Dylan and the people on the outside from coming into the bunker. An explosion in the systems level kills Anders (Erik Svedberg-Zelman), the architect of the bunker, and injures Agent Robinson (Krys Marshall).
The only option now is to implement “Exodus,” the evacuation protocol.
When Sinatra returns to the tower, or control center, she tells Dr. Torabi (Sarah Shahi) and everyone else to go help people evacuate and that she “would take it from here.”
In a call with Dr. Chase, Sinatra tells him the reactors are melting, to close the tram tunnel and “put ‘Alex’ on an island ... ‘Alex’ needs to be protected at all costs.”

Xavier and Teri go in search of their kids, but Presley (Aliyah Mastin) is still stuck in an elevator near the reactors with Sinatra's daughter Hadley (Kate Godfrey). Xavier goes to the tower to search for them on the security cameras and finds Sinatra, who helps him locate the girls.
The two attempt to save their daughters and they are able to with the help of Dylan and his crew, who also made it inside the bunker.

Dylan, with his knowledge of “Alex” and technology, tries to stop the meltdown from happening. In the process, his member Geiger (Michael McGrady) dies. They successfully save injured Agent Robinson, who had told the late President Cal Bradford’s son, Jeremy (Charlie Evans), to leave her behind and save himself.
As they're exiting the lower levels, Xavier experiences déjà vu, remembering his dream that included Dylan. Dylan stops, points his gun at Sinatra and once again asks, “Where is Alex?”
She tells him that “Alex” is already working and adds, “You’re my son. It’s complicated but you are.”
Ready to shoot Sinatra, Dylan also experiences some sort of flashback where he sees the same memory that Xavier did.
“What is this?!” he yells, with Xavier saying that he doesn’t know either. “But I know Annie. I met her in Graceland.”
He tells Dylan that Annie died giving birth to their baby and that he can take him to her.
Sinatra Makes the Ultimate Sacrifice

Dylan says that when Paradise explodes it will spray radiation out the doors “like a cannon.” The doors were built to withstand a nuclear blast from the outside, which means they should be able to contain one from the inside, Sinatra says.
The only issue is that to close the doors, someone needs to be inside the tower, inside Paradise.
Sinatra tells the group to take her to the tower. When her daughter asks where she’s going, Sinatra replies, “To make things right.”

“Alex” had predicted that Sinatra would be dead by the end of the day. Viewers recall that the message stated that it would need a "user" to activate her death. It so happens that the message reads: “Awaiting user: X.”
Sinatra realizes that she will die when Dylan calls Xavier “X.”
In the tower, Sinatra tells Xavier that she will “go down with the ship,” ultimately sacrificing herself to save the others. She also hands him something.

As Paradise collapses, Sinatra goes out to the town square. She is greeted by her late son Dylan, who holds her hand and the two are seen walking down the street. If this is real or an illusion, it’s unclear.
Where Is Xavier Going Next?
The mountain explodes, but where is Xavier?

Thankfully, he’s seen returning to the basecamp where everyone was evacuated and reuniting with his family. Dylan is also seen holding his baby girl and asks Xavier what her name is. He decides to name her Annie.

A flashback shows that Sinatra, before she closed the doors, gave Xavier message that "Alex" predicted. She tells him that he is user X and that there is a second bunker that is deep underneath the Denver airport.
In it, he will find Alex, the quantum computer that “can stop all of this. In fact, it already has stopped all of this.”
She tells him he needs to get there, follow the instructions and “go save the world.”
When Xavier asks what makes her think he will do what she says, she replies, “I believe you already have.”
The episode ends with a view at the second underground bunker where “Alex” is housed and Xavier looking out into the distance.
